title: Preparing for 800 VDC Data Centers: ABB, Eaton Support NVIDIA's AI Infrastructure Evolution
claim_supported: Eaton unveiled an 800VDC reference architecture at OCP Global Summit 2025 (Q1 2026 white paper planned); ABB announced NVIDIA collaboration on 1MW racks/multi-gigawatt campuses (DC breakers, protection relays, busways); NVIDIA's own roadmap targets '~2027' for 800V DC deployment (Vera Rubin NVL144 / MGX). No named datacenter-operator deployment commitments found in this article.
notes: US-leg competitor/timing context for direct-DC power-conversion demand; confirms multiple incumbent shelf-vendors (Eaton, ABB, plus GE Vernova/Delta/Vertiv per prior atlas L02-037) are already moving into the 800VDC conversion space that C-21's multiport DC-DC block would sit behind/beside.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: US trade-media publisher (Endeavor Business Media, Nashville, TN). Not India.

title: 关于有序推动多用户绿电直连发展有关事项的通知 (Notice on Orderly Promoting Multi-User Green Power Direct Connection Development), 发改能源〔2026〕688号
authors_or_org: National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC); National Energy Administration (NEA), China
claim_supported: Issued 2026-05-14 (published 2026-05-20). Quote: "优先支持算力设施、绿色氢氨醇等新兴产业和未来产业开展绿电直连" (priority support for computing facilities and green hydrogen/ammonia/methanol to pursue green-power direct connection). Sets a dated compliance ramp: self-generated/self-consumed electricity must be >=30% of total consumption generally and >=35% before 2030 specifically ("占总用电量的比例不低于30%、2030年前不低于35%"), and annual grid-uploaded electricity must not exceed 20% of available generation.
locator: Notice body, provisions on prioritized industries and consumption-ratio requirements; fetched directly 2026-07-13
notes: This is a distinct, more specific and more recently dated primary CN policy instrument than atlas L14-036 (the April 2026 AI-energy action plan); together they give two independent official CN documents naming green-power-direct-connection-to-computing-load as a named policy lane with an explicit 2030 compliance date, directly supporting C-21's CN chapter (documented as license-out only, not a beachhead).

institutions.name: National Development and Reform Commission / National Energy Administration (China)

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Official Chinese central-government domain (ndrc.gov.cn). No India connection.

title: 'Rogue' communication devices found on Chinese-made solar power inverters
claim_supported: Reuters-sourced reporting (published 2025-05-15) names Huawei Technologies ("the largest maker") and Sungrow among Chinese inverter/battery manufacturers whose equipment has been found, per two unnamed US energy officials, to contain undocumented communication devices/cellular radios capable of circumventing utility firewalls; notes the US federal government has prohibited Huawei inverters/infrastructure equipment since 2019; a Nov 2024 incident saw Chinese-made solar inverters in the US and elsewhere remotely disabled from China.
locator: Article body, paragraphs naming Huawei and Sungrow and describing the 2019 Huawei prohibition and Nov 2024 incident; dated 2025-05-15
notes: Directly names Sungrow (one of C-21's CN named buyer/incumbent ecosystem) alongside Huawei in an active US national-security scrutiny episode; strengthens both (a) the case that C-21's CN leg must remain license-out only and (b) that de-risking from Chinese inverter/BESS suppliers is an active US buyer tailwind for a non-Chinese direct-DC vendor.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: US trade-press publisher (Industry Dive, Washington DC). Not India.

title: OpenSanctions search: 'Sungrow' against the US BIS Entity List (EL) dataset
claim_supported: No matching entity was returned for 'Sungrow Power Supply Co' when searched against OpenSanctions' aggregated US-BIS-EL (Entity List) and related sanctions datasets as of 2026-07-13. This is a negative-finding compliance check, not proof of clean status -- OpenSanctions is a third-party aggregator, not the official BIS system of record, and does not capture every possible affiliate/subsidiary name variant.
locator: Search results page, 'No matching entities were found' message; accessed 2026-07-13
notes: Used only as a documented due-diligence check per mission rule 4 (check named CN customers against BIS Entity List/VEU status). Huawei's confirmed Entity List status (since 2019, per P3R2-C-21-S03) is the more decisive compliance finding for C-21's CN chapter; Sungrow's absence from this aggregator is inconclusive and should not be read as a clean-bill finding given the same outlet (S03) names Sungrow in an active 2025 congressional/DHS security-scrutiny episode.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: OpenSanctions is operated by Data and Research Center gGmbH, Berlin, Germany. Not India.

title: EU harmonised accelerated stress testing protocols for low-temperature water electrolyser
claim_supported: Published 2024-06-13 by the EU's own science service. The document 'introduces proposed accelerated stress testing (AST) protocols' and 'broad guidelines for establishing AST procedures for stacks' across alkaline, AEM, and PEM electrolysers, positioned for 'R&D and stack prototype qualification' -- i.e. a first EU-level harmonization proposal, not a pre-existing settled international standard.
notes: RE-SOURCES the adjudication-flagged self-inference (idea record's 'standards-gap' claim) with an independent, EU-government primary source: as of mid-2024 only a proposed harmonization framework existed, confirming no settled commercial acceptance/degradation-test standard was in force -- consistent with, and now evidentially supporting, C-22's core wedge thesis rather than an unverified inference.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: European Commission science service (Brussels/Ispra). Not India.

title: Electrolyser Test Stations (ETS Series, up to 4MW)
claim_supported: Greenlight Innovation (fuel-cell/electrolyser test-equipment supplier since 1996, 15+ years in electrolyser test stands) offers ETS-series test stations up to 4MW / 30,000A / 1000V+ for PEM, AEM, and alkaline electrolysers, testing cell voltage/pressure/temperature, exhaust-gas flow, and offering optional impedance spectroscopy/voltammetry/leak detection; describes itself as 'the world's leading supplier of test stands for hydrogen electrolyser research.'
notes: Direct, named merchant incumbent in C-22's test-equipment category (general R&D/durability test stands) -- confirms a real competitor exists for generic electrolyser test benches, sharpening C-22's wedge claim to specifically be programmable-ripple/renewable-profile-playback/financier-facing degradation datasets, not test-stand hardware per se, where Greenlight does not appear to compete based on this page.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Greenlight Innovation Corp. is headquartered in Burnaby, British Columbia, Canada. Not India.

title: DOE Restores $1.2B for DAC Hubs and 5 Hydrogen Projects
claim_supported: Published 2026-04-20. DOE's April 2026 preserved-award list (sent to House Appropriations Committee) retains 5 of the original 7 Regional Clean Hydrogen Hubs -- HyVelocity (Gulf Coast/TX, up to $1.2B; Chevron, ExxonMobil among sponsors), ARCH2 (Appalachian, up to $925M), MachH2 (Midwest, up to $1B), MACH2 (Mid-Atlantic, up to $750M), Heartland (up to $925M) -- almost $5B combined -- while ARCHES (California) and the Pacific Northwest Hub were cancelled in Oct 2025.
locator: Article body, hub-by-hub list and dollar figures; dated 2026-04-20
notes: REFRESHES the 2026 baseline: confirms US H2Hubs demand is real but reduced (5/7 survive, ~$5B vs original ~$7B envelope) as of this session -- directly updates atlas L11-032 (which only covered the original Nov 2024 award, pre-dating the 2025 cuts and 2026 restoration).

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: US energy trade-press outlet. Not India.

title: 两部门：加快氢能与绿色燃料产业发展 2030年可再生能源制氢规模达到200万吨 (Two ministries: accelerate hydrogen/green-fuel industry development, 2030 renewable-hydrogen production target of 2 million tonnes)
authors_or_org: National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC); National Energy Administration (NEA), China; reported by STCN (Securities Times)
claim_supported: Published 2026-06-25. Reports NDRC/NEA's 'New Energy System Construction 15th Five-Year Plan' (新型能源体系建设"十五五"规划) reaffirming a dated national target: "2030年可再生能源制氢规模达到200万吨" (renewable-energy hydrogen production capacity to reach 2 million tonnes by 2030), alongside industrial by-product hydrogen purification and green hydrogen-ammonia-methanol production bases nationally. Traces to the original NDRC/NEA 'Hydrogen Industry Medium- and Long-Term Development Plan (2021-2035)' issued 2022-03-23 (ndrc.gov.cn, independently confirmed this session for issuing date/body only, full target text not text-extractable from that page).
locator: Article body, quoted target sentence; dated 2026-06-25
notes: Primary/official CN 2030-dated trigger for C-22's China leg, current as of this session (reaffirmed mid-2026, not a stale 2022 figure) -- pairs with the buyer-side tender evidence (atlas L11-050/L11-051) to give the CN leg both a named-buyer demand signal and an official government-plan timing trigger.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Securities Times is a Chinese state-affiliated financial newspaper (Shenzhen); reporting on official NDRC/NEA policy. Not India.

title: Commonwealth Fusion Systems Installs First SPARC Magnet, Eyes 2026 Plasma Milestone
claim_supported: Published 2026-03-11. CFS installed TF1, the first of 18 HTS magnets (24 tonnes each, 20T field, >30,000A, -253C), in the Tokamak Hall at Devens, MA, with the remaining 17 arriving fortnightly; targets first plasma in 2026 and net fusion energy demonstration in 2027; ARC (grid-scale commercial device) is targeted for 'the early 2030s.'
locator: Article body, magnet installation and ARC-timeline paragraphs; dated 2026-03-11
notes: Second, independent 2026-dated timing source for D-01, corroborating atlas L03-035 (TechCrunch, April 2026, same magnet-buildout program) and giving the clearest near-term hardware milestone (18-magnet buildout in progress at exactly the facility where quench protection would be qualified) plus an explicit early-2030s ARC commercial trigger.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: US-based science/technology news outlet. Not India.

title: TAPESTAR contactless critical-current scanning products
claim_supported: THEVA's TAPESTAR is a non-contact instrument producing 'continuous scans of the critical current along the tape with millimeter resolution' and detecting local faults/non-uniformities in the superconducting coating; THEVA states 'many laboratories and all well-known tape producers rely on this method'; a TAPESTAR XL model targets production-line incoming-goods inspection. THEVA is itself both a REBCO tape producer (Pro-Line) and the instrument vendor.
notes: Confirms the idea record's own competitor claim ('THEVA Tapestar-class -- judgment' in competition_outlook_2030) with a direct, named, fetched primary vendor source -- upgrades that competitor claim from judgment to confirmed. Crucially, THEVA is a tape maker selling its own QC tool, not an independent/buyer-trusted third-party acceptance-metrology vendor, which supports D-02's 'no neutral referee' wedge framing, though it also shows the specific contactless-Ic-scanning technique itself is not novel/undefended IP.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: THEVA is headquartered in Ismaning, Germany. Not India.

title: 致！全球唯二且第一的上海超导及16亿合同来源 (Shanghai Superconductor: one of only two global producers, and the source of its RMB 1.6 billion contract)
authors_or_org: Eastmoney Caifuhao (东方财富网财富号)
claim_supported: Published 2026-05-22. Shanghai Superconductor is one of only two global producers of >1,000km/yr REBCO tape and holds >80% Chinese domestic market share; quote: "上海超导是中科院/中核HL-4（环流四号）的独家高温超导带材供应商，签了16亿元长单" (Shanghai Superconductor is the exclusive HTS-tape supplier for the CAS/CNNC HL-4 tokamak, having signed a RMB 1.6 billion long-term contract covering approx. 3,000km of tape for 2025-2027 delivery). States capacity plan: 4,000km/yr (2025, achieved), +6,000km/yr under construction (2027-28), 20,000km/yr long-term target (2030).
locator: Article body, contract and capacity paragraphs; dated 2026-05-22
notes: Direct, named, dated CN buyer_procurement-grade demand evidence for D-02's China leg (a specific fusion-magnet tape order backed by a named RMB value), independent of and complementary to atlas sources on WST/ASIPP (L03-031/029/037) and the Shanghai HTS cluster (L03-041/052); also the clearest volume-growth datapoint for the 'tape volume triples-to-10x' launch_2030_timing_thesis (4,000km/yr in 2025 -> 20,000km/yr target by 2030).

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Eastmoney is a Shanghai-listed Chinese financial-media/data company. Not India.

title: Klystron Power Amplifiers (KPAs) -- GEN IV product line
claim_supported: CPI's commercial GEN IV klystron power amplifiers (K4C, K4S, K4C8/K4D8/K4U8 TouchPower, up to 3.35kW) incorporate multi-stage depressed collector (MSDC) technology and target satellite Direct-to-Home (DTH) uplink/broadcast markets, not the scientific/accelerator klystron product family (CPI separately makes SLAC/DOE-class multi-megawatt tubes). No efficiency percentage or price figures are published on this specific page.
notes: Confirms CPI as a real, commercially shipping MSDC-klystron incumbent (satcom line), corroborating the idea record's 'CPI (L05-050)' competitor claim, but shows CPI's commercial MSDC expertise is concentrated in satcom tubes, not yet a merchant retrofit-MSDC product for the accelerator/fusion-gyrotron tube classes D-07 targets -- consistent with D-07's 'merchant retrofit modules... unoccupied' framing for that specific sub-market.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: CPI is headquartered in Palo Alto, California, US. Not India.

title: Design and high-power test of an 800-kW UHF klystron for CEPC
authors_or_org: Ou-Zheng Xiao et al., Institute of High Energy Physics, Chinese Academy of Sciences, in collaboration with KEK (Japan)
claim_supported: Published online 2022-07-18 in Chinese Physics B. A 650MHz klystron for CEPC (China's proposed Circular Electron Positron Collider) reached 804kW peak power at ~65.3% efficiency (40% duty cycle; ~700kW at CW due to ceramic-window damage), developed at IHEP/CAS with KEK (Japan) collaboration 'to reduce the energy demand and operation cost' of CEPC. Per WebSearch-corroborated (not independently fetched this session) IEEE/CEPC-TDR reporting, subsequent CEPC klystron R&D reached ~77% efficiency at 800kW against an 80% design goal, framed against a 55% conventional-klystron baseline with cited annual savings of RMB 90-130 million at CEPC's ~60MW total RF load.
locator: Abstract and results text, fetched directly 2026-07-13 (efficiency-progression figures beyond the 2022 paper are WebSearch-only and flagged as such, not independently fetched)
notes: This is CN 'access-limited' demand-logic evidence per the idea record's own framing (CEPC construction is not yet approved/funded; this is a real, named, academically-documented efficiency R&D program, not a booked commercial order) -- used only for the D-07 bottom-up efficiency/savings arithmetic and CN context, NOT as a CN beachhead or market-sizing input, consistent with the idea's china_beachhead=false flag.

institutions.name: Institute of High Energy Physics, Chinese Academy of Sciences
